title: Adventurer, an Origins Addon
description: Adds an additional Origin Layer for your worlds, granting additional depth within your player builds
body:
become an adventurer, and search the world of its hidden powers.

![Adventurer Banner](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/114bdd17b9675cdbd5d6e0bda6b30f6a52d68620_0.webp)

# about the pack

, minecraft somewhat lacks satisfying progression and a customizable build system. this addon adds the Adventurer, allowing crafting of various runes that give passive abilities. The Atlas will tell you the rest.

_, dont forget to download the resourcepack_

![Adventurer Title](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8f60ada99f2f02d68b3c2a901d88f82f3278f490_0.webp)
![Adventurer GUI](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6f1512269b39c5c05a7f2279763dedfa0a55bf4c.png)

### advancements

<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>
, below is some technical info about the pack

### controls
, runes can be equipped by holding on main-hand and **right-click**, unequip all runes at once with **load toolbar key**, and toggle visibility of mana bar with **save toolbar key**

### how does mana work and regenerate
, mana is restored by 4 per sec, 8 when standing still, crystals are meant for important events hence you get only 3 per night which quickly grants 60 mana with right-click

, runes can drain or consume a chunk of mana 

, drains either 0, 2, 4, 6, or 8 mana / consumes chunks of mana when rune condition is met

### is there a way to get the atlas back
, can be crafted, similar to runes

### is this compatible with custom origins
, **right-click** and **save/load toolbar key** are the only keybinds used for this mod, it should be compatible with most custom origins that don't have **excessive keybinds**

### i want to know every single rune, i want to get the advancements
, all crafting recipes and mobs that may drop runes are in the atlas. currently, there are **11 basic**, **19 uncommon**, **15 special**, **4 stunning** and **5 undefined** runes to collect

### datapack + resourcepack variant ?
*-* download, extract, and place the files inside in their respective folders ( datapack / resourcepack )

</details>
